title: Bodhi's Big Backyard Adventure
synopsis: When Bodhi loses his favorite toy airplane in the backyard, he embarks on a big adventure with his sister Maya and neighbor friends to find it, discovering that the best adventures happen right at home.

pages.text: Bodhi loved his red airplane. He threw it high in the air every day. "Zoom! Whoosh!" he would say as it flew around the backyard. But today was different. Bodhi threw his airplane as high as he could. It flew over the fence and disappeared! "Oh no!" cried Bodhi. "My airplane is gone!"

illustration.description: A young South Asian boy with dark hair and big expressive eyes looking shocked and worried, standing in a sunny backyard with green grass and a wooden fence. His hands are reaching up toward the sky where a small red toy airplane is visible flying over the fence. The scene has warm, bright colors with a playful cartoon style.
illustration.alt: Bodhi watching his red airplane fly over the backyard fence

pages.text: Bodhi's big sister Maya came outside. She had curly black hair and wore a yellow dress. "What's wrong, Bodhi?" she asked. "I lost my airplane!" Bodhi said sadly. "It went over the fence into Mrs. Chen's yard." Maya smiled. "Don't worry. We can ask Mrs. Chen if we can look for it. She's very nice."

illustration.description: Maya, a girl with beautiful curly black hair wearing a bright yellow dress, kneeling down next to her younger brother Bodhi who looks sad. They are in their backyard with flowers and trees in the background. Maya has a kind, reassuring expression while Bodhi looks up at her hopefully. Rich saturated colors with a warm, comforting mood.
illustration.alt: Maya comforting her brother Bodhi in the backyard

pages.text: The two children walked to the front yard. Mrs. Chen was watering her beautiful garden. She had gray hair and kind eyes behind her glasses. "Hello, Bodhi and Maya!" she said with a big smile. "Mrs. Chen," said Bodhi politely, "my airplane flew into your backyard. May we please look for it?" "Of course!" said Mrs. Chen. "Let's go on an adventure together!"

illustration.description: An elderly Asian woman with gray hair and glasses, wearing a blue gardening apron, smiling warmly while holding a watering can. Colorful flowers bloom around her in the garden. Bodhi and Maya stand nearby looking hopeful. The scene is bright and cheerful with vibrant flower colors and morning sunlight filtering through.
illustration.alt: Mrs. Chen in her garden talking to Bodhi and Maya

pages.text: Mrs. Chen's backyard was like a jungle! There were tall sunflowers, a big apple tree, and a small pond. "Wow!" said Maya. "Your yard is amazing!" Mrs. Chen laughed. "Thank you! Now, where could that airplane be?" They looked behind the sunflowers. No airplane. They looked under the apple tree. No airplane there either.

illustration.description: A magical backyard garden with towering yellow sunflowers, a large apple tree with red apples, and a sparkling blue pond. Mrs. Chen, Bodhi, and Maya are searching among the plants. The children look amazed by the beautiful garden. Butterflies flutter around and the scene has rich, saturated colors with dappled sunlight creating a whimsical atmosphere.
illustration.alt: The group searching through Mrs. Chen's magical garden

pages.text: Just then, two more children appeared. "Hi!" said a boy with freckles and red hair. "I'm Sam from next door. What are you looking for?" Behind him was a girl with braids and a purple shirt. "I'm Zara," she said. "We saw you looking around." "We're looking for my red airplane," explained Bodhi. "Can you help us?" "Yes!" said Sam and Zara together. "Let's find it!"

illustration.description: Two new children joining the group - Sam, a boy with bright red hair and freckles wearing a green t-shirt, and Zara, a Black girl with neat braids wearing a purple shirt. All five characters are gathered together in the garden, with excited expressions. The scene shows friendship and teamwork with warm, inviting colors and a sense of adventure.
illustration.alt: Sam and Zara joining the search party in the garden

pages.text: The five friends searched everywhere. Sam looked in the tall grass. Zara checked near the pond. Maya climbed up to look in the apple tree. Mrs. Chen moved flower pots. Bodhi looked under the garden bench. "I found something!" called Zara. Everyone ran over. But it was just a red leaf, not the airplane. "Don't give up," said Mrs. Chen. "Good things come to those who keep trying."

illustration.description: All five characters actively searching different areas of the garden. Zara is holding up a red leaf with a slightly disappointed but still hopeful expression. The other children are gathered around her. The garden setting is lush and detailed with various hiding spots visible. The mood is determined and optimistic despite the momentary disappointment.
illustration.alt: Zara holding up a red leaf while the group continues searching

pages.text: Mrs. Chen had an idea. "Let's think like an airplane," she said. "If I were flying and the wind caught me, where would I go?" She pointed to the very back of her yard. There was a small shed with a green roof. "The airplane might be behind the shed!" said Maya. They all walked over together. Their hearts were beating fast with excitement.

illustration.description: The group walking together toward a charming small garden shed with a mossy green roof. Mrs. Chen is pointing toward it with an excited expression. All the children look eager and hopeful, walking in a line behind her. The shed is surrounded by climbing vines and flowers, with late afternoon sunlight creating long shadows across the garden path.
illustration.alt: The search party heading toward the garden shed

pages.text: Behind the shed, they found a small vegetable garden. There were tomatoes, carrots, and lettuce growing in neat rows. And there, sitting perfectly on top of a big pumpkin, was Bodhi's red airplane! "There it is!" shouted Bodhi happily. He ran over and picked up his favorite toy. "Thank you, everyone!" he said. "I couldn't have found it without you!"

illustration.description: A delightful vegetable garden with neat rows of green plants, orange pumpkins, and red tomatoes. Bodhi's bright red toy airplane sits prominently on top of a large orange pumpkin. Bodhi is reaching for it with pure joy on his face while the other characters watch with big smiles. The scene is filled with warm afternoon light and celebrates the moment of discovery.
illustration.alt: Bodhi discovering his airplane on top of a pumpkin in the vegetable garden

pages.text: Mrs. Chen smiled and picked some fresh vegetables. "Take these home to your family," she said. "And remember, you're always welcome in my garden." Bodhi hugged his airplane tight. "This was the best adventure ever!" he said. Sam, Zara, Maya, and Mrs. Chen all agreed. Sometimes the biggest adventures happen right in your own neighborhood. And the best part was making new friends along the way.

illustration.description: All five characters standing together in a group hug or celebration, with Mrs. Chen holding a basket of fresh vegetables. Bodhi is clutching his red airplane happily. Everyone has big smiles and the garden around them is glowing in warm golden hour light. The scene conveys friendship, community, and the joy of adventure. Butterflies and birds add to the magical, heartwarming atmosphere.
illustration.alt: The whole group celebrating together in the garden with vegetables and the found airplane

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What color was Bodhi's airplane?
questions.explanation: Bodhi's airplane was red. The story mentions his 'red airplane' several times.

questions.question: Who is Bodhi's big sister?
questions.explanation: Maya is Bodhi's big sister. She wore a yellow dress and had curly black hair.

questions.question: Where did they finally find the airplane?
choices: Behind the shed on a pumpkin
questions.explanation: They found the airplane sitting on top of a big pumpkin in Mrs. Chen's vegetable garden behind the shed.

questions.question: What did Mrs. Chen give the children at the end?
questions.explanation: Mrs. Chen picked fresh vegetables from her garden and gave them to the children to take home to their families.

questions.question: How many friends helped search for the airplane?
questions.explanation: Four friends helped: Maya (his sister), Mrs. Chen, Sam, and Zara. Including Bodhi, there were five people total searching.

pages.title: Complete the Adventure
pages.instructions: Fill in the missing words from the story!
pages.text: Bodhi threw his _____ airplane high in the air. It flew over the _____ and into Mrs. Chen's yard. His sister _____ helped him look for it. They searched behind the sunflowers and under the _____ tree. Finally, they found it sitting on a big _____ in the vegetable garden!

pages.title: Garden Search
pages.instructions: Help remember what they found in Mrs. Chen's garden!
pages.text: Mrs. Chen's backyard was like a _____! There were tall _____, a big apple tree, and a small _____. Sam and _____ came to help search. Mrs. Chen said, 'Good things come to those who keep _____.' They found the airplane behind the _____ in the vegetable garden.
